rubric_items.reasoning: The deployment is a genuine, root-cause solution rather than a papered-over pass: a 3-node StatefulSet with persistent (5Gi PVC) storage, correct discovery via the headless service, HTTP TLS sourced from the provided es-http-tls secret, and a self-generated transport-TLS secret (a necessary and well-reasoned addition since xpack.security is implied by the provided elastic-password secret, and ES 8.x requires transport TLS when security is enabled). Init containers fix data-dir ownership and vm.max_map_count, both real prerequisites for ES pods to start reliably. Verification wasn't just an oracle-satisfying probe: cluster health is green with 3/3 data nodes, and an actual write/read/search round-trip succeeded with the index showing active primary+replica shards distributed across nodes, confirming genuine multi-node durability rather than a single-node fluke.

The visible tail of the transcript shows a coherent, non-repetitive verification sequence: write → read → search → per-index cluster health → PDB/endpoints/statefulset/pod inventory → targeted cleanup of the test index and scratch files. There's no sign of contradictory or repeated failed attempts in what's shown, and the submission summary lines up cleanly with the tool outputs. Scored just under 1.0 only because the log head (where the initial diagnosis and resource authoring happened) is truncated and not fully visible to confirm zero backtracking end-to-end.

Commands were targeted and least-privilege: scoped `kubectl get`/`exec` reads for verification, reuse of the pre-existing curl-test pod instead of spinning up new debug infrastructure, and a scoped cleanup (deleting only the test index and its own scratch files) rather than broad or destructive operations. No force flags, no unnecessary broad deletes, no mutations outside the elasticsearch namespace.

Only 6 mutations were needed to stand up the entire cluster: ConfigMap, headless Service, HTTP Service, PDB, StatefulSet, and the transport-cert Secret — essentially the minimal native-resource footprint for a secured 3-node ES cluster under the no-CRD/no-operator constraint. Nothing extraneous was created, and test artifacts (index, scratch cert files) were cleaned up afterward, leaving no collateral state.
